questions.solution: Step 1: Preserve meaning in fewer words. Step 2: 'He failed because he did not study.'. Answer: He failed because he did not study..

questions.question: Condense: 'In spite of the fact that he was ill, he attended the lecture.' (Set 99)
options: Because he was ill, he attended.
He was ill, so he attended.
He attended because illness.
Although he was ill, he attended the lecture.
questions.solution: Step 1: Preserve meaning in fewer words. Step 2: 'Although he was ill, he attended the lecture.'. Answer: Although he was ill, he attended the lecture..

questions.question: Condense: 'He is so rich that he can buy anything.' (Set 100)
options: He is rich but cannot buy.
He is too rich to buy.
He buys because poor.
He is rich enough to buy anything.
questions.solution: Step 1: Preserve meaning in fewer words. Step 2: 'He is rich enough to buy anything.'. Answer: He is rich enough to buy anything..
